script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'champion-pipeline-dossier',
  description: 'Document the full 0.4447 champion pipeline end-to-end (query->search->corpus->outline->write->verify->assemble->score) with token cost and wall-clock time',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Investigate' },
    { title: 'Synthesize' },
  ],
}
const WT = '/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent'
const SP = '/tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad'

phase('Investigate')

const stages = [
  { key: 'search', prompt: `READ-ONLY. cd ${WT}. Document STAGE 1-2: QUERY GENERATION + MULTI-SOURCE SEARCH of the champion's corpus-building pipeline. The corpus (data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json) was built by the SWEEP run 'SWEEP_workforce_drb_72_ai_labor' (see the corpus '_provenance'). Read src/agents/planner_agent.py (how it generates sub-queries from the RQ), src/agents/search_agent.py (which sources: Semantic Scholar/PubMed/arXiv/Serper Google; the QueryAmplifier at src/search/query_amplifier.py), src/orchestration/iteration_manager.py (max_iterations=15, convergence/stopping), src/orchestration/dynamic_replanner.py (gap-driven re-query). Document EXACTLY: how a query is generated, which sources are hit and how, how results iterate (the "search and search" loop), the stopping criteria, and any config (max iters, concurrency limits, amplification). Give file:line for each claim. Also find the actual sweep command / entry point (real_corpus_run*.py) and its parameters.` },
  { key: 'corpus', prompt: `READ-ONLY. cd ${WT}. Document STAGE 3: CORPUS CONSOLIDATION. The sweep output (cp3, s3_consolidate) was converted by scripts/cp3_to_cp4_corpus.py into data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json. Read that converter and the corpus. Document: the corpus STRUCTURE (top keys: research_question, domain, evidence[997], finding_clusters[329], same_work_groups, basket_total, _provenance), the per-evidence fields (authors, direct_quote, statement, doi, journal, tier, provenance_class, v30_entity_id), the QUALITY TIER system (T1-T7, UNKNOWN) — what each tier means and how tiers are assigned, and the corpus STATS: 997 evidence items across 841 distinct works, median direct_quote ~2527 chars. Explain how finding_clusters and same_work_groups are computed and used. file:line for each.` },
  { key: 'outline_write', prompt: `READ-ONLY. cd ${WT}. Document STAGE 4-5: OUTLINE AGENT + SECTION WRITING. Entry: generate_multi_section_report (src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py) with PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1; the outline agent is src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py (the React loop: build outline, auto-assign/fold evidence to sections, search_more_evidence, checklist gaps, finish_outline / BOUNCED / convergence ~9 turns). Then the parallel SECTION WRITERS that produce FREE PROSE via glm-5.2 (find the write prompt + how evidence baskets map to a section + [CITE:ev_xxx] provenance tokens). Document the exact flow, the convergence mechanism, how free-writing differs from a template renderer, model config (glm-5.2, temperature, max_tokens). file:line + quote the actual write-prompt.` },
  { key: 'verify_assemble', prompt: `READ-ONLY. cd ${WT}. Document STAGE 6-7: strict_verify (POST-HOC FAITHFULNESS) + ASSEMBLY. Read src/polaris_graph/generator/verified_compose.py and the strict_verify path. Document: how each generated sentence is verified against its cited evidence, how [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens resolve to a numbered [N] bibliography, the drop-and-regenerate-on-failure logic (a real run: 86 sentences verified / 96 dropped), the faithfulness audit (0 leaked tokens, all [N] resolve, faithfulness_pass=True). Then ASSEMBLY (scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py ~line 249-340): sections + derived title + abstract + methods + bibliography + evidence table -> report.md. Read the actual 0.4447 report docs/step3_insight_lever_ab/control_report.md and characterize its prose quality (flowing, cites, evidence table). file:line.` },
  { key: 'score', prompt: `READ-ONLY. cd ${WT}. Document STAGE 8: RACE SCORING. The scorer is third_party/deep_research_bench/deepresearch_bench_race.py + prompt/criteria_prompt_en.py + score_prompt_en.py. Document: how RACE works (an LLM judge = openai/gpt-5.5 scores BOTH the target report AND a human reference report against per-task weighted criteria; Overall = target/(target+reference)), the 4 dimensions (Comprehensiveness, Insight, Instruction-Following, Readability) and their task-72 weights, what each criterion rewards. Then the ACTUAL 0.4447 result: results/race/polaris_step3_control/race_result.txt (Comp 0.4569, Insight 0.4293, IF 0.4587, Read 0.4310, Overall 0.4447). Note the reference is claude-3-7-sonnet (~0.42). file:line + exact numbers.` },
  { key: 'cost_time', prompt: `READ-ONLY. cd ${WT} and also check /home/polaris/wt/flywheel. Document TOKEN COST + WALL-CLOCK TIME for the whole champion pipeline. Hunt for cost ledgers (logs/pg_cost_ledger.jsonl, PG_COST_LEDGER_PATH, any *cost*.jsonl) and sum input/output/reasoning tokens and $ per stage. For TIME: the corpus SWEEP duration (cp3 run logs / _provenance created_utc vs sweep start), the compose elapsed (outputs/*/compose_summary.json 'elapsed_seconds' — a real run was ~1207s), the RACE score duration (control_race.log timestamps). Produce a per-stage AND total breakdown: (a) total tokens (in/out/reasoning), (b) total USD cost, (c) total wall-clock (mining + compose + score). If exact numbers aren't fully recoverable, give best estimates with the evidence and clearly mark them as estimates. Be concrete with file:line and actual figures.` },
]

const findings = await parallel(stages.map(s => () =>
  agent(s.prompt, { label: `doc:${s.key}`, phase: 'Investigate' })
))

phase('Synthesize')

const report = await agent(
  `You are writing the DEFINITIVE technical dossier of the "champion" deep-research pipeline that scored RACE Overall 0.4447 on DeepResearch Bench task 72. Six investigators documented the stages; their findings follow. Produce ONE comprehensive, polished, well-structured markdown report suitable for a technical/executive audience (a Telus AI meeting). 

Structure it as:
1. Executive summary (what the pipeline is, the 0.4447 result, the one-line "why it wins").
2. End-to-end flow diagram in text (Query -> Search -> Corpus -> Outline -> Write -> Verify -> Assemble -> Score) with a one-line description of each stage.
3. Stage-by-stage deep detail (each of the 8 stages: what runs, key files, config, the actual mechanism, concrete numbers).
4. The corpus (997 works, 841 papers, tiers, 2527-char passages) and WHY quality-of-evidence matters.
5. The scoring (RACE mechanics, the 4 dimensions + weights, the 0.4447 breakdown vs the reference).
6. COST & TIME: a clean table of tokens + USD + wall-clock, per stage and total.
7. Why it beats the alternatives (1 sentence on the cellcog contrast: write-freely-verify-after vs template-render).
Be precise, use the real numbers and file references from the findings, use tables where helpful, and write cleanly. Do NOT invent numbers — if a figure was marked an estimate, keep it marked.

=== STAGE 1-2 QUERY+SEARCH ===
${findings[0]||'(failed)'}
=== STAGE 3 CORPUS ===
${findings[1]||'(failed)'}
=== STAGE 4-5 OUTLINE+WRITE ===
${findings[2]||'(failed)'}
=== STAGE 6-7 VERIFY+ASSEMBLE ===
${findings[3]||'(failed)'}
=== STAGE 8 SCORE ===
${findings[4]||'(failed)'}
=== COST+TIME ===
${findings[5]||'(failed)'}`,
  { label: 'synthesize-dossier', phase: 'Synthesize', effort: 'high' }
)

return { report }

result.report:
# The Champion Deep-Research Pipeline — Technical Dossier

**RACE Overall 0.4447 on DeepResearch Bench Task 72 (AI's impact on the future labor market)**
Worktree of record: `/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent` · Corpus run id `SWEEP_workforce_drb_72_ai_labor_1783476454`

---

## 1. Executive Summary

This pipeline is an **evidence-first, verify-after deep-research system**. It mines a domain corpus of real papers, builds an agentic outline that reasons over a corroboration-basket digest, writes free-flowing synthetic prose section-by-section, and then subjects **every single sentence** to a fail-closed, span-grounded faithfulness gate before assembly. Nothing reaches the final report that did not survive a per-sentence numeric + entailment check against a specific byte-range of a cited source.

On **DeepResearch Bench Task 72**, scored by an `openai/gpt-5.5` RACE judge against the `claude-3-7-sonnet` reference, the target report earned:

| Dimension | Weight | Normalized Score |
|---|---|---|
| Comprehensiveness | 0.29 | 0.4569 |
| Insight | 0.32 | 0.4293 |
| Instruction-Following | 0.25 | 0.4587 |
| Readability | 0.14 | 0.4310 |
| **Overall** | — | **0.4447** |

**Why it wins in one line:** the writer is given *freedom to synthesize* but is *bounded downstream by verification* — 64 of 155 candidate sentences (~41%) were dropped rather than shipped unverified, so the report reads like a genuine literature review while every quantitative claim is a traceable, span-grounded slice of a real source.

> Note on parity: all four normalized dimensions sit below the 0.5 parity line, so gpt-5.5 judged this report as somewhat weaker than the reference on this task — but **0.4447 clears the ~0.42 band the claude-3-7-sonnet reference scores in its own self-comparison run**.

---

## 2. End-to-End Flow

```
[1] QUERY      Planner turns the research question into 20–30 perspective-tagged
               sub-questions; amplifier fans each into ~10 search variants.
                     │
[2] SEARCH     Multi-source fan-out (OpenAlex, Semantic Scholar, Serper Scholar/
               News/Patents, PubMed, Exa, DuckDuckGo) with domain-diversity dedup.
                     │
[3] CORPUS     cp3 consolidation → deterministic cp3→cp4 join: 997 evidence rows,
               329 corroboration baskets, 55 same-work groups, T1–T7 tiers.
                     │
[4] OUTLINE    ReAct outline agent (glm-5.2) reasons over the basket digest, runs
               3 gap detectors into one GapLedger, converges by gap-exhaustion (~9 turns).
                     │
[5] WRITE      4–7 sections written in PARALLEL (glm-5.2), free prose, one hard rule:
               every sentence ends in an [ev_XXX] marker from its assigned evidence.
                     │
[6] VERIFY     strict_verify: per-sentence span-grounded numeric + entailment gate;
               fail-closed cascade drops any sentence that doesn't trace to its span.
                     │
[7] ASSEMBLE   Kept sentences resolved to numbered [N] bibliography; sections + intro
               + limitations + references concatenated to report.md; leak tripwire audits.
                     │
[8] SCORE      RACE (gpt-5.5 judge): target vs reference on 4 weighted dimensions
               → overall = target/(target+reference) = 0.4447.
```

---

## 3. Stage-by-Stage Deep Detail

### Framing caveat (important)
The corpus was **not** built by the legacy LangGraph orchestrator (`src/orchestration/`, "pipeline C"), which has been **FROZEN since 2026-03-16** (retirement signed off 2026-04-18) and cannot even instantiate in this worktree — it imports a non-existent `src.depth.depth_config` (`ModuleNotFoundError`). The live corpus-builder is **pipeline A** (`src/polaris_graph/`), whose searcher *reuses the leaf search functions and the amplifier* from the frozen code but runs its own LangGraph loop. Where numbers below come from the frozen design vs. the live path, this is flagged.

### Stage 1 — Query Generation
- **Planner** (`planner_agent.py:174`, `_process_initial_planning:541`): one structured LLM call (`ResearchPlan`) using the STORM prompt, asking for **20–30 analytical sub-questions**, each tagged with one of **9 STORM perspectives** (Scientific, Regulatory, Industry, Economic, Public_Health, Historical, Regional, Methodological, Emerging_Trends) plus 3–5 search keywords. Config: `task_tier="important"`, `temperature=0.1`, `max_tokens=8000`.
- **Query validation** (`_validate_queries:430`): FIX-124C topic anchoring (keep if relevance true or overlap ≥ 0.1) then FIX-46 embedding filter — `all-MiniLM-L6-v2`, cosine ≥ **0.3** to the original query, never dropping below 5 queries.
- **Amplification** (`query_amplifier.py:131`): each query → deduplicated variants (original + exact-match, 7 academic suffixes, regional patterns, `site:` operators, temporal variants). **Live pipeline A caps at `original_count × 3`** (`PG_AMPLIFICATION_VARIANTS=3`, `state.py:80`).
- **Gap-driven re-query** (iteration ≥ 1): appends `Gap_Filling` sub-queries rather than replacing.

### Stage 2 — Multi-Source Search
Frozen `search_agent.py` registers 9 tools; the **live academic breadth** in pipeline A comes from a superset:

| Source | How hit | Live in Pipeline A |
|---|---|---|
| OpenAlex | `api.openalex.org/works` (`PG_OPENALEX_MAX_PER_QUERY=10`, 474M works) | **primary academic**, `searcher.py:166` |
| Semantic Scholar | `graph/v1/paper/search/bulk`, x-api-key, ≤3000 results | reused |
| Serper Scholar / News / Patents | `google.serper.dev/*` | reused |
| PubMed / NCBI | E-utilities esearch→efetch | reused |
| Exa neural search | `searcher.py:361` | **new** |
| DuckDuckGo | fallback | reused |

**arXiv is NOT a dedicated source** (docstring/rate-limit stubs only; reached indirectly via URL). Execution fans out **web vs academic** buckets: web in `ThreadPoolExecutor` (pipeline A `PG_WEB_CONCURRENCY=20`), academic at **concurrency 1** (`PG_ACADEMIC_CONCURRENCY=1`, `PG_MAX_TOTAL_ACADEMIC=100`). URL dedup → domain diversity (cap 10/domain) → perspective-aware merge. A pre-fetch authority gate (`PG_AUTHORITY_GATE=0.3`) replaced the old domain blocklist (removed 2026-04-13).

> Loop constants (`max_iterations=15`, 45-min wall, novelty-0.05, coverage-0.85) belong to the **frozen** pipeline C; pipeline A instead bounds work by turns and wall-seconds at the outline stage.

### Stage 3 — Corpus Consolidation
`scripts/cp3_to_cp4_corpus.py` is a **deterministic, offline join** producing `data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json` (5.8 MB). It joins **cp3** (`s3_consolidate` baskets, keyed by resolved `member_evidence_ids`) with the **cp2** evidence pool (996 `evidence_for_gen` rows + 1 demoted `ev_621`). It **fails closed**: every referenced id must resolve or it raises rather than write a partial corpus. See §4 for the full corpus anatomy.

### Stage 4 — Outline Agent (ReAct loop)
`src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py` (2572 lines). Kill-switch `PG_OUTLINE_AGENT` (default OFF → byte-identical legacy pass-through; ON runs the agentic loop).
- **Seed** via legacy `_call_outline` using the **code model** `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro`; state packed into an `OutlineWorkspace`, checkpointed after every mutation.
- **Loop** (`run():1950`): while `turn < PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS=24` and `elapsed < PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_WALL_SECONDS=900`. Each turn = ONE glm-5.2 structured `ReactDecision` (`reasoning_effort="high"`) choosing one action from `{analysis tools | inspect_basket | search_more_evidence | update_outline | list_tools | finish_outline}`.
- **Three gap detectors → one `GapLedger`** (deduped by (section, aspect), Jaccard-0.4 paraphrase collapse): (1) per-section checklist self-review — every deficiency must carry a *verbatim quote from the research question* (anti-invention gate); (2) deterministic tool-failure check; (3) agent-initiated new-aspect record. Per-aspect retry cap `PG_OUTLINE_GAP_RETRIES_PER_ASPECT=2`; exhausted gaps become UNFILLED + disclosed, never silently dropped.
- **Convergence = gap-ledger exhaustion**, not a fixed turn count. `finish_outline` accepts only when the checklist names no new deficiencies AND `pending_count == 0`. The champion own-corpus path converges in **~9 turns**. On exception/timeout it degrades to seed, never to the plain outliner. Telemetry writes `cp4_used = "agentic"`.
- **Faithfulness invariant:** the outline agent manufactures ZERO evidence text and marks nothing "verified" — every folded row is a real fetched-and-classified row.

### Stage 5 — Parallel Section Writers (free prose)
`multi_section_generator.py` (12083 lines). Sections generated **concurrently** (`asyncio.Semaphore`, default 3, `PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS`), each crash-isolated and wall-clock bounded.
- Each section pulls exactly the rows the outline agent assigned (`ev_subset = [pool[id] for id in section.ev_ids]`). Empty sections ship a visible gap-stub, never vanish.
- **Model = glm-5.2** (`PG_GENERATOR_MODEL=z-ai/glm-5.2`). `temperature=0.3` (→0.1 on tighter retry); content `max_tokens` floored above the reasoning pool (`PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS=64000`, `PG_SECTION_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS=16384`).
- **The write contract is free prose, not a template.** The system prompt's hard rules: *use ONLY facts in the `<<<evidence:ev_XXX>>>` blocks; EVERY sentence must end with ≥1 `[ev_XXX]` marker; verbatim numbers, no rounding; attribute superlatives; target 10–18 sentences; ≥5 distinct sources.* There is no fixed sentence skeleton — the model synthesizes across converging sources and decides framing itself. (Contrast the separate slot-based `run_contract_section` renderer — *not* the path used here.)
- **Token flow:** outline agent assigns `ev_ids` → writer free-writes `[ev_XXX]` → span-rewrite stamps `[#ev:ev_XXX:start-end]` → strict_verify re-checks the digits/claim against that byte-range. Verified compute goes through a separate fail-closed `[#calc:...]` lane.

### Stage 6 — strict_verify (post-hoc faithfulness)
`provenance_generator.py` — `strict_verify:3598`, `verify_sentence_provenance:2098`. Findings are sentence-split, pre-filtered (chrome/boilerplate removed from *both* numerator and denominator), then each surviving sentence runs a **fail-closed cascade** (any failure reason ⇒ drop):

1. No `[#ev:...]` token ⇒ drop. 2. Evidence id must exist in pool. 3. Span in bounds. 4. Cited span can't be a CAPTCHA/cookie/404 shell. 5. Contentless residue dropped. 6. **Numeric match (union across multi-cite):** every decimal and standalone integer must appear in some cited span; percents must appear in a cited span. 7. Binding-qualifier retention (dropped hedges fail). 8. Content-word overlap ≥ threshold. 9. Named-trial match. 10. **Entailment/NLI leg** — LLM judge; NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED drops under `enforce`; a fail-open judge is tracked and re-gated fail-closed.

**Drop-and-regenerate:** if `post_filter_fraction < min_kept_fraction` (compose call site sets **0.4**), regenerate the section ONCE with a `tighter_retry` hard-contract reminder; if still thin, the section is `dropped_due_to_failure`. Every downstream "lever" is written to be **faithfulness-neutral** — it changes *which* verified span is surfaced, never a verdict.

### Stage 7 — Assembly
`scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:249–394`. `generate_multi_section_report(...)` (agentic outliner pinned, `min_kept_fraction=0.4`, `section_temperature=0.3`) → kept sentences resolved via `resolve_provenance_to_citations` into first-appearance-ordered `[N]` markers and a bibliography row per source. Assembly = derived title + fixed topic-neutral framing intro + `## {title}` sections (non-dropped, non-empty) + limitations + `[N] statement — url (tier Tx)` references → `report.md`. **Methods is a sidecar (`methods.md`), explicitly NOT judged.** An independent tripwire audits `report.md`: `leaked [CITE:ev_xxx] == 0` AND no unresolved `[N]` markers ⇒ `faithfulness_pass`; process exit code is `0 iff faithful`.

**The actual 0.4447 report** (`docs/step3_insight_lever_ab/control_report.md`): 9 thematic H2 sections + Limitations + References, ~3230–3914 words, flowing dense synthetic prose. Every quantitative claim carries an inline `[N]` marker, sometimes stacked (`[7][3]`) where a basket has multiple corroborating sources. A visible fingerprint of the verbatim-span engine: occasional doubled periods before a citation (`…impact.[1].[2]`) where a quoted span ends and the resolver appends `[N]`. **37 distinct `[N]` markers resolve exactly to 37 references; faithfulness_pass=True, zero leaked tokens.**

### Stage 8 — RACE Scoring
See §5.

---

## 4. The Corpus — Why Quality-of-Evidence Matters

`data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json` — 7 top-level keys, verified from the file:

| Key | Value |
|---|---|
| `research_question` | AI's impact on the future labor market (academic research before June 2023) |
| `domain` | `workforce` |
| `evidence` | **997** items |
| `finding_clusters` | **329** corroboration baskets |
| `same_work_groups` | **55** |
| `basket_total` | 329 |
| `_provenance` | cp3_run_id `SWEEP_workforce_drb_72_ai_labor_1783476454`, pool_size 997, referenced_ids 425 |

**Per-evidence fields** include `direct_quote` (the verbatim span — the load-bearing faithfulness anchor), `statement` (short claim/title, median 60 chars), `authors`, `doi`, `journal`, `tier`, `source_url`, `year`, `pmid`.

**Corpus stats:**
- **997 evidence rows** (996 `evidence_for_gen` + 1 supplemented `ev_621`).
- **Distinct works:** the pipeline-reported figure is **841**; measured directly the corpus has **856 distinct raw titles / 851 normalized**, folding to ~890 works at runtime. Identity rests on title/URL, not DOI — **DOIs are almost entirely absent (5 of 997 rows)**.
- **`direct_quote` length:** median **2527 chars** (mean 4540, range 27–24915) — large multi-paragraph spans, not one-liners. *This is why verification can work:* long spans give the numeric-match and entailment gates real text to bind against.
- **Cluster sizes:** 291 singletons, 22×size-2, 14×size-3, 2×size-4 (385 memberships across 329 clusters). **same_work_groups:** 55 groups over 154 rows.

**The T1–T7 tier system** (`tier_classifier.py`):

| Tier | Meaning (field-agnostic; workforce mapping in brackets) |
|---|---|
| T1 | Peer-reviewed primary study [peer-reviewed economics papers] |
| T2 | Systematic review / meta-analysis |
| T3 | Government / regulatory body [BLS / OECD / StatCan statistical releases] |
| T4 | Narrative review, commentary, editorial |
| T5 | Industry-funded report |
| T6 | News / blog / non-peer-reviewed web |
| T7 | Abstract-only / stub (<~1000 chars) |
| UNKNOWN | Rules could not decide — user review required (distinct from a positive low-tier label) |

Assigned upstream at fetch time (carried through verbatim), dispatched by `classify_source_tier`, defaulting to LLM tiering (GLM-5.2, the I-wire-001 bake-off winner, +0.353 macro-F1) with deterministic rules as fallback. **Tier is always a weight, never a drop.**

**Observed distribution (997 rows):** UNKNOWN 253, T6 207, T4 204, T3 181, T7 84, T1 42, T5 21, T2 5 — a web-heavy, mostly DOI-less AI-labor corpus.

**Why quality-of-evidence matters:** Task 72's instruction is to *cite only high-quality, English-language journal articles* — and two RACE instruction-following criteria (0.25 combined weight) directly reward source discipline. The corroboration baskets let the writer prefer multi-work agreement over single-source claims, the same-work groups prevent one paper's mirrors from inflating corroboration, and the tier weighting biases surfacing toward T1–T3. The large UNKNOWN/T6 share is precisely the honesty tax the pipeline discloses in its Limitations section (6% T1, 21% T6, 15% UNKNOWN).

---

## 5. The Scoring — RACE Mechanics

**RACE** (Reference-based, Adaptive Criteria-driven Evaluation), driver `deepresearch_bench_race.py`:
- **Judge = `openai/gpt-5.5`** (`utils/api.py:47`). Sampling params intentionally unset (gpt-5.x reasoning models reject non-defaults).
- **Both articles in one prompt:** `article_1 = target`, `article_2 = reference` (always `claude-3-7-sonnet`, `reference.jsonl`). The judge scores each article on every criterion (0–10 continuous), returning JSON keyed by the 4 dimensions.
- **Per-task criteria + weights are themselves LLM-generated** (`criteria_prompt_en.py`, each criterion set forced to sum to 1.0).

**Formulas:**
```
dimension_avg   = weighted mean of criterion scores
target_total    = Σ (dimension_avg × dimension_weight)
overall_score   = target_total / (target_total + reference_total)
normalized[dim] = target_dim / (target_dim + reference_dim)
```
A report identical to the reference scores **0.5**.

**Task-72 dimension weights:** Insight **0.32**, Comprehensiveness **0.29**, Instruction-Following **0.25**, Readability **0.14** — an insight-heavy analytical literature-review task. Instruction-Following's two citation-discipline criteria (high-quality journals 0.15, English-language 0.10 = 0.25 combined) directly reward the source firewall.

**The 0.4447 breakdown** (`results/race/polaris_step3_control/race_result.txt`, task 72 only):

| Dimension | Weight | Normalized | Full precision |
|---|---|---|---|
| Comprehensiveness | 0.29 | 0.4569 | 0.45686900958466453 |
| Insight | 0.32 | 0.4293 | 0.4292604501607717 |
| Instruction-Following | 0.25 | 0.4587 | 0.45869947275922673 |
| Readability | 0.14 | 0.4310 | 0.4310051107325384 |
| **Overall** | — | **0.4447** | 0.44470339333234166 |

- **Weakest = Insight (0.4293)** — costly because it carries the highest weight (0.32).
- **Strongest = Instruction-Following (0.4587) and Comprehensiveness (0.4569)** — the source-discipline levers paying off.
- **Readability (0.4310)** is low but only weighted 0.14, so it drags Overall least.

**The reference (~0.42):** claude-3-7-sonnet's own self-comparison run scores Overall **0.4218** (Comp 0.4110, Insight 0.4051, IF 0.4621, Read 0.4172). Values near 0.42 there reflect judge/run variance rather than the definitional 0.5 midpoint. The key point: **0.4447 > the ~0.42 reference band overall**, though still under the 0.5 parity line on this task.

---

## 6. Cost & Time

**Models:** agent + generator `z-ai/glm-5.2`; code `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro`; RACE judge `openai/gpt-5.5`.

**Ledger mechanics:** the pipeline LLM cost ledger is `logs/pg_cost_ledger.jsonl` (per-worktree; `cumulative_cost_usd` resets per compose run — the final row is authoritative). **Mining does not hit the ledger** (pure HTTP retrieval; ~$0 LLM). **RACE scoring does not hit the ledger** (separate gpt-5.5 harness) — its cost is an *estimate*.

### Pipeline B — `champ_ourcorpus` (clean, authoritative isolation)

| Stage | Tokens (in / out / reasoning) | USD | Wall-clock |
|---|---|---|---|
| MINE (retrieve) | 0 LLM (HTTP only) | ~$0 (API) | ~6 min active fetch; elapsed hours (rate-budget waits) |
| BUILD corpus (evidence extraction → 838 baskets) | 667,425 / 239,320 / 82,839 | **$1.6371** | ~40 min |
| COMPOSE | 630,208 / 237,525 / 35,540 | **$1.5661** | **1207.8 s (20.1 min)** |
| SCORE (RACE) | off-ledger (gpt-5.5) | ~$0.5–2 *(est)* | ~60–130 s/report |
| **TOTAL** | **1,297,633 / 476,845 / 118,379** (~1.89M I/O + 0.12M reason) | **$3.20 ledger + ~$1 RACE (est) ≈ $4.2** | active compute ≈ **28 min**; full elapsed with corpus-build + rate waits ≈ several hours |

Report output: 4,842 words, 83 bib entries, 11 sections, 8 agent turns, faithfulness pass.

### Pipeline A — `rank12_tierfirst_compose` (committed champion, s3gear_329 corpus)

| Stage | Tokens (in / out / reasoning) | USD | Wall-clock |
|---|---|---|---|
| MINE + BUILD s3gear_329 (329 baskets) | pre-built 07-11→07-12 | included in outline lifetime | assembled over ~hours (heavy iteration) |
| COMPOSE | ~2.71M / 0.65M / 0.18M *(window polluted by concurrent runs)* | **≈$3.0–3.4** (best est **~$3.2**) | **3067.3 s (51.1 min)** |
| SCORE (RACE) | off-ledger (gpt-5.5) | ~$0.5–2 *(est)* | 68 s/it |
| **TOTAL** | ~3.5M I/O + ~0.2M reason (compose, est) | **~$3.2 ledger + ~$1 RACE ≈ $4–5** | ~**52 min** active (mining excluded) |

Report output: 7,939 words, 107 bib entries, 10 sections, 11 agent turns; RACE overall 0.4313.

> **Note:** the RACE-**0.4447** report is the `polaris_step3_control` run (`step3_control_compose.log`: elapsed 1671.0 s, 9 sections, 3230 words, 91 verified / 64 dropped, in_tok 324,734 / out_tok 39,965, drop rate ~41%). Pipelines A and B above are the two on-disk champion *instances* used to characterize cost; the 0.4447 control run sits between them in scale.

**Bottom line:** COMPOSE is the cost/time core ($1.57 / 20 min for 4.8K words up to ~$3.2 / 51 min for 7.9K words — cost scales with length/turns). Corpus BUILD is comparable to a compose (~$1.64). MINE is ~$0 in LLM tokens but the wall-clock long pole in *elapsed* terms (API rate budgets, hours — not compute). SCORE is off-ledger, ~1–2 min/report. **Champion single-run ≈ $3.2 LLM (ledger-verified) + ~$1 RACE (est) ≈ $4–5, ~1.9M I/O tokens, ~28–52 min active compute** (excluding multi-hour mining rate-limit waits).

*Estimate flags retained:* RACE token/$ (no ledger, extrapolated from race logs); rank12 compose $ (window pollution, bounded $3.0–3.4); mining elapsed (active fetch measured, total inflated by OpenAlex rate-budget deferrals).

---

## 7. Why It Beats the Alternatives

The champion **writes freely and verifies after** — the section writer synthesizes continuous prose across converging sources, then a fail-closed per-sentence gate drops anything unverifiable — whereas the contrasting **cellcog template-render** approach fills fixed slots from structured extraction, buying provenance safety at the cost of the cross-source narrative synthesis that RACE's Insight and Comprehensiveness dimensions (0.61 combined weight on Task 72) actually reward.
